root_text.sn35.177-179:1.1: “Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ, tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.177-179:1.3: Rūpā, bhikkhave, aniccā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.177-179:1.4: Saddā aniccā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.177-179:1.5: Gandhā aniccā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.177-179:1.6: Rasā aniccā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.177-179:1.7: Phoṭṭhabbā aniccā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.177-179:1.8: Dhammā aniccā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.177-179:1.9: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn35.177-179:0.3: Desire, Etc. for the Impermanent Exterior
translation_text.sn35.177-179:1.1: “Mendicants, you should give up desire … greed … desire and greed for what is impermanent.
